jaxlibxc
  • API Reference
  • Numerical Safety
  • View page source

Numerical Safety

Numerically safe operations for JAX autodiff. All functions ensure both branches of jnp.where produce finite values, which is critical for gradient computation.

Numerically safe operations for JAX autodiff.

All functions are designed so that both branches of jnp.where produce finite values, which is critical for JAX gradient computation (both branches are always evaluated, even if only one result is selected).

jaxlibxc._numerical.my_piecewise3(cond, val_true, val_false)[source]

Differentiable piecewise: if cond then val_true else val_false.

Both val_true and val_false MUST be finite for all inputs. This is the JAX translation of libxc’s my_piecewise3.

jaxlibxc._numerical.my_piecewise5(cond1, val1, cond2, val2, val3)[source]

Five-argument piecewise: if cond1 then val1 elif cond2 then val2 else val3.

Translation of libxc’s my_piecewise5.

jaxlibxc._numerical.safe_pow(x, n)[source]

x^n with clamped base to avoid NaN gradients at x=0.

jaxlibxc._numerical.safe_log(x)[source]

log(x) with clamped argument.

jaxlibxc._numerical.safe_sqrt(x)[source]

sqrt(x) with clamped argument.

jaxlibxc._numerical.safe_div(x, y)[source]

x/y with denominator clamped away from zero.
